description: Note *This model is not available in 3DEC . As discussed previously in the CYSoil model , when soils are subjected to deviatoric loading, they usually exhibit a decrease in stiffness accompanied by irreversible deformation. The well-known Duncan and Chang model (1970) is commonly used to simulate this hyperbolic stress-strain behavior. The Duncan and Chang model is relatively easy to use. However, as noted in in the CYSoil model , this model has several drawbacks. In addition, hyperbolic relations that rely on nonlinear elasticity are known to have significant limitations (Duncan et al. 1980),

property_groups.name: Properties
property_groups.description: Material properties for the 'cap-yield-simplified' zone constitutive model. Assign the model with 'zone cmodel assign cap-yield-simplified [range ...]', then set these with 'zone property <keyword> <value> [range cmodel-name cap-yield-simplified]'.

properties.description: dimensionless bulk modulus reference value, K_{ref}
ultimate cohesion, c
ultimate dilation angle, \psi_f
ultimate friction angle, \phi_f
mobilized friction angle, \phi_m, which should be initialized no less than the value determined by this equation via a command or FISH function
current Poisson’s ratio, \nu
initial mean effective pressure, p_m, which should be initialized via a command or FISH function
reference pressure, p_{ref}. A non-zero value should be specified based on the unit of stress/pressure adopted in the model.
dimensionless shear modulus reference value, G_{ref}
tension limit, \sigma^t. The default is 0.0.
dimensionless Young’s modulus reference value, E_{ref}
current mobilized dilation angle, \psi_m. It is read-only, except for table-dilation ǂ 0.
If true, the tension limit is set to 0 in the event of tensile failure. The default is false.
= 0 for mobilized dilation angle, \psi_m, equal to input value, dilation or a function of plastic shear strain if table is input with table-dilation
bulk modulus exponent, m. The default is 0.5.
shear modulus exponent, n. The default is 0.5.
initial mobilized fiction angle, \phi_0 associated with zero plastic shear strain. By default, \phi_0 = \phi_m if strain-shear-plastic is initialized to 0; otherwise, \phi_0 = 0 if not specified.
constant used in dilation law (flag-dilation = 1 or 2), \phi_{cv}
accumulated plastic shear strain, \epsilon^{ps}. By default, it is initialized internally.
accumulated plastic tensile strain, \epsilon^{pt}. The default initial value is 0.
failure ratio, R_f. The default is 0.9.
name of the table relating cohesion, c, to plastic shear strain
name of the table relating mobilized dilation angle to plastic shear strain
name of the table relating mobilized tensile strength to plastic tensile strain
current elastic bulk modulus, K^e
current elastic shear modulus, G^e
current elastic Young’s modulus, E
